title: Bindings, Scope, Closures, and Lifetime · Language Lens: Let Const Var
summary: Build configurable counters and validators without leaking mutable state across community-service widgets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the js let const var increment in a closure-backed state and validation library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.
Use BigInt and Symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
